name: Dover-Phila
description_short: Dover-Phila Federal Credit Union is a community-based credit union established in 1953, serving over 33,900 members in Dover, Ohio with $679M in assets.
description_long:
Dover-Phila Federal Credit Union was established in 1953 and is headquartered in Dover, Ohio. As a federally-chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative serving the Dover community and surrounding areas. The institution has grown to steward over $679 million in assets and maintains the distinction of being the 12th largest credit union in Ohio.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of banking products and services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), IRAs/KEOGH accounts, auto loans, personal loans, and business loans. Additional services include bill pay, online loan payments, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, e-statements, wire transfers, financial calculators, financial counseling, financial education workshops, and electronic signature authentication. Members can access accounts through 1 branch location and 5 ATMs.

Dover-Phila positions itself as a community-focused financial institution with over 92 dedicated employees as of 2026. The credit union provides membership eligibility for specific geographic communities to enhance financial accessibility for local residents and businesses. Account types include regular savings (46,800+ accounts), share draft/checking (26,600+ accounts), money market (3,900+ accounts), and share certificates/CDs (4,500+ accounts).

However, member reviews reveal significant concerns about service quality and responsiveness. Recent reviews highlight fraud issues with inadequate resolution support, loan application rejections without personal communication, and refusal to provide in-office debit card services. One member reported being directed to competitor banks due to poor service. The current member rating stands at 1.0/5 based on available reviews, indicating substantial dissatisfaction with customer service practices and relationship management. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Bill pay and online loan payments
Mobile payments and remote deposit capture
pros: Established 1953 with strong community history and $679M in assets
Comprehensive product suite including auto loans, personal loans, and business loans
Multiple account types available (savings, checking, money market, CDs, IRAs)
24/7 online and mobile banking access with bill pay and remote deposit capture
12th largest credit union in Ohio with 33,900+ members
Financial counseling and educational workshops available to members
ATM network with 5 locations plus 1 branch for access
cons: Critically low 1.0/5 member rating based on available reviews indicating severe service dissatisfaction
Multiple fraud complaints with reports of unhelpful resolution and poor support
Slow loan processing and rejection via mail without personal communication or relationship consideration
Refusal to provide in-office debit card services, requiring 10-day wait periods
Limited branch presence with only 1 physical location in Dover, OH
best_for: Dover, Ohio residents seeking local credit union membership with community banking
Individuals with established relationships at the credit union seeking basic banking services
Members needing multiple account types (savings, CDs, IRAs) in one institution
Local business owners requiring business loan and personal banking services
diagnosis: Dover-Phila Federal Credit Union is best for Dover, Ohio residents who prioritize geographic proximity and community banking roots, and who have simple financial needs (savings, checking, basic loans). However, the institution carries a significant caveat: recent member reviews indicate serious deficiencies in customer service, fraud support, and relationship management, with a critically low 1.0/5 member rating suggesting customers should carefully weigh alternatives before joining or conduct direct outreach to assess whether service issues have been addressed.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
